# Transcrição: 24. Trait 23 - Great Salespeople know how to listen more than they talk.

- **Fonte:** 24. Trait 23 - Great Salespeople know how to listen more than they talk.
- **Duração:** 01:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:18]

Great salespeople know how to listen more than they talk. They know how to use the two ears and the one mouth. But don't get confused. Great salespeople don't spend three and four and five hours listening to people. They're not psychologists that sit there and listen and then give advice. Okay? The great salespeople know how to go in, probe, collect data. When they have that vital data, pull it out and use it. Right? They're like seals. They're like they're dropped into a mission. They're not a get in, achieve their mission and get out. That's what they do with their listening skills. Learn how to listen like the greats so that you get the prime data you need, that really important data, those dominant buying motives. Why someone would do something and pull out and then use that data. Great salespeople know how to collect data very, very quickly, rapidly, have full intention of listening to their customer and then know when it's enough and determine what's the best product for that client and how to present that client so that the client is able to say, bing, bing, bing, bing, everything important to me. Let's roll. That's what great students know how to listen more than they talk and when they talk they know how to take what they listened, use it and close the deal.
